evidence: State/view-model objects are rebuilt as large mutable aggregates without stable identity (e.g., `imageEditValue` in `useInlineEditState` is reconstructed inline and then provided via context).

Memoization is neutralized by depending on freshly-created object containers (`props` and `state`) in `useSegmentSlotModeData`, while those containers are recreated each render in `useSegmentSlotMode`.
Task payload assembly is repeated across multiple modules with many conditional spreads and mixed canonical/legacy branches, creating drift-prone field handling instead of a shared mapper contract.
Join Clips keeps both canonical and compatibility entrypoints active, with duplicated boundary validation/shape resolution across `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` and `joinClips.ts`.

Edge shared helpers expose multiple incompatible public contracts for similar failure paths: `ensureRequestMethod` returns `Promise<Response | null>` while `parseJsonBody` returns `Promise<OperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>>` in `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts`, and `checkRateLimit` returns a fail-open `RateLimitResult` with `degraded` markers in `supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts`.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` and `supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts` use wide positional APIs with many same-typed parameters (`checkOrchestratorCompletion(...)`, `markOrchestratorComplete(...)`, `triggerCostCalculation(...)`), increasing call-site ambiguity.

Join-clips services mix throw-based, sentinel-value, and silent-empty contracts: `toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput` throws validation errors (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts`), `consumePendingJoinClips` catches and returns `[]` (`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipInitService.ts`), and `loadClipDuration` catches and returns `durationSeconds: 0` (`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipManager/metadata.ts`).

Multiple deprecated compatibility surfaces remain active in assigned files: `getLegacySupabaseClient` (legacySupabaseFacade), `reportRuntimeError` alias (handleError), `createJoinClipsTaskCompat` + legacy fields (joinClipsCompatAdapter), and legacy travel structure field types (legacyStructureVideo).

`joinClipsCompatAdapter` enforces removal target for legacy clip-path mode (`assertLegacyClipSourceAllowed`) but still accepts legacy `video_edit_mode` + `source_*` payloads with no analogous sunset gate.
`useCredits.ts` is a dedicated deprecated shim re-export, indicating old and new import surfaces still coexist.

`src/shared/hooks/` is overloaded (81 production files) and mixes broad concerns; assigned files include a deprecated billing shim (`useCredits.ts`), theme hook (`useDarkMode.ts`), and generic timing hook (`useDebounce.ts`) in one flat package.

`src/shared/components/` is also overloaded (70 files with mixed production/test), and assigned files include both production components (`AuthGate.tsx`, `AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx`) and a colocated test (`BulkEditControls.test.tsx`), while the repo also maintains `src/shared/components/__tests__/` (35 files), indicating inconsistent test boundaries.

For assigned symbols, import paths show migration drift: consumers still import `@/shared/components/AuthGate`, `@/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer`, and `@/shared/hooks/useDarkMode`, while duplicate implementations now exist in domain folders (`src/shared/auth/components/`, `src/shared/components/generation-controls/`, `src/shared/hooks/theme/`).

`useDebounce` has very low practical fan-in from app code (single observed production consumer: `src/pages/Home/HomePage.tsx`), which matches the packet’s straggler heuristic (fan-in <5) and suggests feature-level placement rather than shared-root placement.

Generation/task access patterns are inconsistent: `src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts` scopes task queries with `.eq('project_id', projectId)`, while `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` performs update/delete/star mutations by `id` only.

`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` exposes `projectId` in mutation arg types (e.g. update mutation) but does not apply it in Supabase filters, indicating partial/unfinished scope enforcement.

`src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts` recursively fetches variants by raw `variantId` with `.eq('id', variantId)` and no project/user boundary check, unlike `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` which has explicit project-scope mismatch states.

findings.summary: Inline edit state builds a huge context value object per render, forcing broad rerender churn and making behavior harder to reason about.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` constructs `imageEditValue` as a large inline object (`lines 344-421`) without `useMemo`, then returns it.
`InlineEditView` passes that object to `<ImageEditProvider value={state.imageEditValue}>` (`line 109`), so any parent re-render creates a new provider value identity.
Downstream lightbox/layout code consumes image-edit context heavily; unstable provider identity widens rerender scope across overlay/tooling components.
findings.suggestion: Extract a dedicated selector-based context model or memoize `imageEditValue` with stable callbacks/segments (`canvas`, `drawing`, `generation`) so provider value identity only changes when relevant slices change.

findings.summary: Segment-slot memoization is effectively bypassed because `useMemo` depends on object wrappers recreated every render.
evidence: `useSegmentSlotModeData` uses `useMemo` with `[props, state, ...]` (`lines 414-421`).
`useSegmentSlotMode` recreates `state` as a new object literal each render (`lines 459-468`), and `props` is a fresh function argument object.
This defeats the intended memo boundary for `segmentSlotModeData`, increasing callback/data identity churn passed into video lightbox mode flows.
findings.suggestion: Stop depending on wrapper objects in memo deps; destructure only primitive/stable dependencies and memoize callback bundles separately (or convert to smaller hooks returning stable slices).

findings.summary: Manual payload mappers implement inconsistent null/optional semantics for the same field set, creating subtle contract drift.
evidence: `travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` only includes `selected_phase_preset_id` when truthy (`lines 208-210`), dropping explicit `null` clears.
`individualTravelSegment.ts` also gates `selected_phase_preset_id` on truthiness (`lines 548-550`).
`joinClips.ts` always writes `selected_phase_preset_id` with fallback/default (`line 321`), so identical conceptual field has different write semantics by task family.
findings.suggestion: Centralize shared payload field semantics in a reusable mapper (including explicit null-pass-through policy), then have each task family compose from that mapper instead of hand-rolled conditional spreads.

findings.summary: Join-clips keeps dual API boundaries (compat + canonical) with duplicated validation/mapping, increasing indirection cost and migration drag.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` duplicates most canonical fields plus legacy ones in a large parallel interface (`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` lines 24-88).
Validation and mode-boundary logic exists in both adapter (`validateCompatModeBoundaries`, `toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput`) and canonical module (`normalizeJoinClipsParams`).
Test coverage remains heavily centered on compat entrypoint (`joinClips.test.ts` describe block and many compat calls), which keeps the legacy seam a first-class dependency.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one public entrypoint: move legacy coercion into a small pre-normalization utility, make canonical input the only exported creation API, and pivot tests to canonical behavior with a minimal legacy fixture set.

findings.summary: Shared edge helpers use incompatible failure contracts for similar request-validation concerns
evidence: `parseJsonBody` returns `OperationResult` (success/failure object) while `ensureRequestMethod` returns `Response | null` in the same module (`edgeRequest.ts`).

`checkRateLimit` never returns a failure object; it fail-opens to `{ allowed: true, degraded: true }` on RPC/runtime errors (`rateLimit.ts`), which differs from both response-returning and result-returning patterns.
`triggerCostCalculation` returns a separate `{ ok: boolean, ... }` envelope (`billing.ts`), creating a third error-contract style for edge utilities.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one shared edge contract for helpers (for example, a discriminated `OperationResult<T>`), then provide thin adapters only at HTTP boundaries (`Response` construction). Standardize method checks, JSON parsing, rate limiting, and downstream fetch helpers on that contract.

findings.summary: Critical orchestration APIs rely on wide positional argument lists with ambiguous string parameters
evidence: `checkOrchestratorCompletion(supabase, taskIdString, completedTask, publicUrl, supabaseUrl, serviceKey)` takes 6 positional params, including three string args with different meanings.
`markOrchestratorComplete(...)` takes 8 positional params and forwards a subset to `triggerCostCalculation(supabaseUrl, serviceKey, orchestratorTaskId, ...)`, compounding ordering risk.

These signatures exceed the batch’s API-shape consistency guideline and are inconsistent with object-parameter patterns already used elsewhere in the same file (e.g., lookup helpers using named option objects).
findings.suggestion: Convert orchestration and billing entrypoints to named options objects (e.g., `{ supabase, taskId, completedTask, urls: { publicUrl, supabaseUrl }, credentials: { serviceKey } }`) and keep backward-compat wrappers temporarily if needed.

findings.summary: Join-clips helpers mix throw, silent-empty, and sentinel-success failure semantics
evidence: `toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput` and compat validation paths throw `TaskValidationError` for invalid inputs (`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts`).
`consumePendingJoinClips` catches broad errors and returns `[]`, which is indistinguishable from a legitimate 'no pending clips' case (`clipInitService.ts`).
`loadClipDuration` catches metadata failures and returns `{ durationSeconds: 0 }`, conflating extraction failure with a real zero-duration value (`metadata.ts`).
findings.suggestion: Standardize join-clips service APIs on a discriminated result (`{ ok: true, data } | { ok: false, errorCode, recoverable }`) and avoid ambiguous sentinel values (`[]`, `0`) for error cases unless explicitly wrapped with status metadata.

findings.summary: Legacy migration enforcement is inconsistent across compatibility surfaces
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter` hard-fails legacy clip-path usage after removal target (`assertLegacyClipSourceAllowed`), but `legacySupabaseFacade` only logs once via `console.error` and continues serving the legacy accessor.
`handleError.ts` keeps deprecated alias exports (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with metadata but no runtime/CI gate to force retirement.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one migration policy: either enforce hard-fail after target or enforce build-time import budgets for all deprecated entry points. Apply the same mechanism to legacy Supabase facade and deprecated error aliases, not only join-clips legacy path mode.

findings.summary: Join-clips still accepts legacy video-edit payload fields without sunset cutoff
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter.resolveVideoEditConfig` still maps `video_edit_mode` + `source_video_*` + `portions_to_regenerate` into canonical `video_edit`.
Removal-target enforcement is implemented for legacy clip-path fields but not for legacy video-edit payload fields, creating asymmetric migration completion.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` shows similar continued legacy-field support in adjacent task payload domain, indicating ongoing dual-schema operation.
findings.suggestion: Add a removal-target gate for legacy video-edit fields parallel to `assertLegacyClipSourceAllowed`, then phase to canonical `video_edit` only. Keep one temporary telemetry period, then remove legacy video-edit fields and update callers/tests.

findings.summary: Deprecated shim entry points remain exported as first-class surfaces
evidence: `useCredits.ts` is a deprecated compatibility re-export module, preserving old import path.
`handleError.ts` exports deprecated alias symbol and alias type (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) alongside canonical API.
findings.suggestion: Demote deprecated shims from normal exports: add strict lint/import-ban rules on legacy symbols, publish direct codemod replacements, and remove shim modules once import budget reaches zero.

findings.summary: Join-clips compat adapter has oversized mixed-shape parameter bag and branchy normalization
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` combines canonical, legacy, and mode-specific fields in one broad interface, then performs extensive branch checks and migration logic in one module.

The adapter contains duplicated/near-duplicated legacy detectors (`hasLegacyVideoEditFields` vs `hasLegacyVideoEditPayloadFields`) and a large legacy-field stripping destructure block, which increases maintenance cost.
Similar broad legacy-field interfaces appear in `legacyStructureVideo.ts`, showing repeated mixed-shape handling style.
findings.suggestion: Split compat handling into explicit translators per legacy shape (clip paths, legacy video-edit payload, canonical) and narrow each input type. Keep one orchestrator function that selects translator, instead of one wide interface and many internal branches.

findings.summary: Repeated inline fallback chains for schema variants reduce local clarity
evidence: `extractSegmentImages` manually resolves multiple schema locations (`individual_segment_params`, top-level, `orchestrator_details`, array offsets) with inline precedence logic.
`getSourceTaskId` hardcodes legacy key-priority fallback chain (`source_task_id` -> `orchestrator_task_id` -> `task_id`).
`getGenerationId` and media URL helpers do similar key-alias fallback chains, repeating low-level compatibility logic in separate utilities.
findings.suggestion: Introduce shared schema-normalization helpers (e.g., canonical media/task param readers) and move key-priority tables to centralized constants so each utility reads normalized fields instead of repeating inline fallback chains.

findings.summary: Core modules have duplicate implementations across old `shared` paths and new domain folders, creating ambiguous ownership boundaries.
evidence: `AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` in `src/shared/components/` and `src/shared/components/generation-controls/` are duplicated implementations.
`AuthGate.tsx` in `src/shared/components/` and `src/shared/auth/components/` are duplicated implementations.
`useDarkMode.ts` in `src/shared/hooks/` and `src/shared/hooks/theme/` both exist, but production imports still target the old shared-root path.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) pick canonical targets (`src/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx`, `src/shared/auth/components/AuthGate.tsx`, `src/shared/hooks/theme/useDarkMode.ts`), (2) update all imports from legacy paths using `rg -l "@/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer|@/shared/components/AuthGate|@/shared/hooks/useDarkMode" src`, then edit to canonical paths, (3) convert legacy files to temporary re-export shims with explicit deprecation comments or remove them once callers are migrated, (4) validate with `npx vitest run src/shared/components/__tests__/AuthGate.test.tsx src/shared/components/__tests__/AspectRatioVisualizer.test.tsx src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: `useCredits` remains as a deprecated compatibility shim in shared root, blurring feature ownership after migration to billing feature hooks.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts` is only a deprecated re-export to `@/features/billing/hooks/useCredits`.
The path is still explicitly allowlisted in legacy governance (`legacySupabaseAllowlist.json`), indicating organizational debt was formalized rather than removed.
Production consumers in sampled files already import billing hook directly, so mixed path ownership is unnecessary for most current callers.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create a compatibility namespace like `src/shared/hooks/compat/` (or `src/shared/legacy/hooks/`) and move `useCredits.ts` there with `git mv`, (2) replace any remaining imports to canonical feature path via `rg -l "@/shared/hooks/useCredits" src | xargs -I{} sed -i '' 's#@/shared/hooks/useCredits#@/features/billing/hooks/useCredits#g' {}`, (3) remove the legacy allowlist entry once no imports remain, (4) validate with `npx vitest run src/features/billing/hooks/__tests__/useCredits.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Component tests are split between `__tests__` and root colocated files, increasing directory noise in an already overloaded shared components package.
evidence: Assigned file `src/shared/components/BulkEditControls.test.tsx` is colocated in production directory root.
Same package also has dedicated `src/shared/components/__tests__/` with 35 tests, so placement conventions conflict.
Packet structure shows `src/shared/components/` already exceeds flat-directory cohesion thresholds (70 files, mixed concerns).
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) standardize on one test boundary for this package (`src/shared/components/__tests__/` or strict colocated-only), (2) if choosing `__tests__`, move `BulkEditControls.test.tsx` first with `git mv src/shared/components/BulkEditControls.test.tsx src/shared/components/__tests__/BulkEditControls.test.tsx`, (3) adjust relative imports in moved tests (e.g., `../BulkEditControls`), (4) enforce with lint rule/glob convention and validate using `npx vitest run src/shared/components/__tests__/BulkEditControls.test.tsx src/shared/components/__tests__/AuthGate.test.tsx src/shared/components/__tests__/AspectRatioVisualizer.test.tsx`.

findings.summary: `useDebounce` is placed in global shared hooks despite low observed fan-in and feature-skewed usage.
evidence: Observed production import usage for `useDebounce` is concentrated in `src/pages/Home/HomePage.tsx`.
The hook sits in `src/shared/hooks/`, a high-volume directory (81 files), where low-fan-in utilities become hard to discover and own.
This matches package straggler criteria from the packet (fan-in <5 suggests non-core placement).
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) decide scope: if truly generic keep in shared but move to `src/shared/hooks/timing/useDebounce.ts`; if feature-specific move to `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDebounce.ts`, (2) move file and its test together (`git mv` both implementation and test), (3) update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/hooks/useDebounce" src`, (4) validate with `npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebounce.test.ts src/pages/Home/HomePage.test.tsx` (or new path equivalent) plus `npx tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Deprecated Supabase facade remains a cross-subsystem dependency hub.
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` is marked deprecated with removal target `2026-06-30` but remains high-fan-in (130 importers).
Structure profile shows `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/` average fan-in 65.5 with heavy consumers outside integrations (`src/shared/hooks/`: 34).
README states legacy usage is governance-gated, but current placement still allows broad direct runtime imports from application packages.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `src/shared/services/supabase/clientAccess.ts` as the only app-layer client accessor wrapping canonical runtime API; (2) migrate imports in order `src/shared/hooks/**` -> `src/shared/components/**` -> `src/tools/**` away from `@/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade`; (3) move legacy files to `src/integrations/supabase/_deprecated/` and block new app-layer imports. Run `rg -n "@/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade" src`, then `desloppify move src/integrations/supabase/legacy src/integrations/supabase/_deprecated --dry-run`, then validate with `npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage && npm run check:contracts && npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Canonical generation/shot types live in a mixed global types package.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` is a top-imported contract hub (205 importers) but is housed in generic `src/types/`.
Directory profile for `src/types/` mixes unrelated concerns (`env.ts`, `database.ts`, `project.ts`, `tasks.ts`, generation/shot types), reducing navigability by ownership boundary.
README compatibility guidance references type-surface migration, but current package shape keeps a central global barrel rather than domain-scoped ownership.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `src/domains/generation/types/` and `src/domains/shot/types/` with explicit domain barrels; (2) move `generationParams`, `generationMetadata`, `generationRow`, and `shot` exports into those folders; (3) keep `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` temporarily as deprecated shim re-export, then remove after import budget reaches zero. Use `rg -n "@/types/(generationAndShots|generationParams|generationMetadata|generationRow|shot)" src`, then `desloppify move src/types/generationAndShots.ts src/domains/generation/types/index.ts --dry-run`, and validate with `npm run check:shots-shim-usage && npm run check:contracts && npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Shared UI primitive package embeds app-theme policy in core button contract.
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `APP_BUTTON_SIZES` and `APP_BUTTON_VARIANTS` from `src/shared/components/theme/buttonVariants` while being a shared `@uiContract` primitive.

UI package (`src/shared/components/ui/`) is large (58 files) and already consumed widely; embedding app theme policy in primitive increases package responsibility mixing and makes the primitive less portable.
`cn.ts` is a pure UI utility in the same folder, highlighting inconsistency between primitive-only and app-policy-coupled files within one package boundary.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) split primitive and app adapters by creating `src/shared/components/ui/primitives/buttonPrimitive.tsx` (base variants/sizes only) and `src/shared/components/theme/adapters/AppButton.tsx` (theme extensions); (2) migrate imports from `@/shared/components/ui/button` to the adapter where app styling is needed, keeping primitive imports for neutral use cases; (3) leave a temporary compatibility export in old path, then remove. Use `rg -n "@/shared/components/ui/button" src`, `desloppify move src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx src/shared/components/ui/primitives/buttonPrimitive.tsx --dry-run`, and validate with `npm run test -- src/shared/components/ui/button.test.tsx && npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Generation/task data access mixes scoped and unscoped authorization boundaries
evidence: `useTaskStatusCounts` consistently filters by `project_id`, but generation mutations and lineage traversal rely on ID-only access.
Repository code includes explicit scope-mismatch handling (`GenerationProjectScopeStatus` / `scope_mismatch`) while sibling hooks bypass equivalent checks.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce one authorization contract for client-side data access: require project scope for mutation/read paths where resource ownership matters, and centralize it in shared repository helpers so hooks cannot issue ID-only queries by default.

findings.summary: Generation mutations allow ID-only write operations without project scoping
evidence: `useDeleteFromTable` deletes by `.eq('id', id)` only for `generations`/`generation_variants`.
`updateGenerationLocation` and `toggleGenerationStar` also update rows via ID-only filters.
The update mutation argument includes optional `projectId` but the query does not use it.
findings.suggestion: Require `projectId` for generation mutations and include `.eq('project_id', projectId)` (or equivalent ownership join/guard). Fail closed when scope is missing, and route writes through a scoped repository API to prevent future bypasses.

findings.summary: Lineage traversal follows variant IDs across generations without ownership scope checks
evidence: `fetchVariantById` reads `generation_variants` by `.eq('id', variantId)` and returns the row directly.
Recursive traversal continues via `params.source_variant_id` without validating project/user scope at each step.
findings.suggestion: Add scoped lineage retrieval (project-scoped or owner-scoped), ideally by joining through generation/project ownership and rejecting traversal when any hop leaves the caller’s authorized scope.
